zh.,Trentham Cart.,Stafford,England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾特倫塔姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Vivian De Standon於大約1202年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯坦登時，他的父親Robert Standon31歲，母親Philippa de Stoke29歲。他於1219年與Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon在英國爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.,Trentham Cart.,Stafford,England: 英国英格兰斯塔福爾特倫塔姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Vivian De Standon于大约1202年出生在英国斯塔福爾斯坦登时，他的父亲Robert Standon31岁，母亲Philippa de Stoke29岁。他于1219年与Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon在英国爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Standon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Standen
meanings: English: habitational name predominantly from Standen in Pendleton (Lancashire) and Standean in Ditchling (Sussex), but also from other places similarly named, including Standen in East Grinstead (Sussex), Standen in Biddenden (Kent), Standen in Benenden (Kent), Upper and Lower Standen in Hawkinge (Kent), Standen (Berkshire, Wiltshire, Isle of Wight), and Standon (Devon, Hampshire, Hertfordshire, Staffordshire). The Lancashire, Hampshire, and Wiltshire placenames, as well as Standean in Ditchling (Sussex), derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + denu ‘valley’; Standen in East Grinstead (Sussex) derives from Old English stān + denu or denn ‘pasture’; Standen in Benenden and Standen in Biddenden (Kent) derive from Old English stānig ‘stony, rocky’ + denn; Standen in Hawkinge (Kent) derives from Old English stān + denn; the Berkshire, Devon, Hertfordshire, Isle of Wight, and Staffordshire placenames derive from Old English stān + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Vivian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Vivian De Standon was born about 1202, in Standon, Staffordshire, England, his father, Robert Standon, was 31 and his mother, Philippa de Stoke, was 29. He married Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon in 1219, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Vivian De Standon est né environ 1202, à Standon, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Standon, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Philippa de Stoke, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon en 1219, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Vivian De Standon um 1202 in Standon, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Standon 31 und seine Mutter Philippa de Stoke 29. Er hat 1219 in Cheshire, England Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Vivian De Standon è nato il circa 1202 nel Standon, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Standon, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Philippa de Stoke, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Lady Rohese de Stafford - Lady Standon nel 1219, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia.
